Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NELSON WILLIAMS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Nelson Williams' motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' complaint is denied without prejudice to defendant raising the issue on a motion for summary adjudication. (See Code Civ. Proc. 437c(f)(1); see Catalano v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 91, 92 ["a claim for punitive damages is one of the substantive areas which is properly the subject of a motion for summary adjudication."].) In general, punitive damages may be awarded on a claim for intentional discrimination in an appropriate case. (See Weeks v. Baker & McKenzie (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1128, 1147-1148 ["The FEHA does not itself authorize punitive damages. It is, however, settled that California's punitive damages statute, Civil Code section 3294, applies to actions brought under the FEHA, including actions brought for sexual harassment."].) To be sure, not every such action warrants such an award. (See, e.g., Turman v. Turning Point of Central California, Inc. (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 53, 63-64.) However, the Court does not believe it is a useful exercise at this early stage of the litigation to parse the allegations of the complaint, which purports to state eleven causes of action on behalf of four different plaintiffs, to determine which allegations meet the statutory standard and which do not. Again, defendant may raise the issue later in the case on a complete factual record.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
